items.title: Bavarian Nordic – Transactions in Connection with Share Buy-Back Program
items.body: Bavarian Nordic A/S has executed transactions under its share buy-back program launched on June 2, 2026, repurchasing 50,169 shares at an average price of DKK 182.35 per share for a total of DKK 9,148,515. The company now holds 2,788,153 treasury shares, representing 3.52% of its share capital.

items.title: Telenor receives approval to acquire GlobalConnect's consumer business in Norway
items.body: The Norwegian Competition Authority has approved Telenor's NOK 6 billion acquisition of GlobalConnect's consumer fibre business, adding approximately 125,000 customers. The deal is conditional on remedies including divesting overlapping infrastructure and opening the acquired network to competitors.
items.author: Benzinga

items.title:
$LULU

Everyone is focused on the guidance cut, but the valuation is getting hard to ignore.

✅ Revenue: $11.2B
✅ Net Income: $1.46B
✅ Free Cash Flow: $1.28B
✅ ROE: 32%
✅ P/E: 9.2
✅ Share count shrinking via buybacks

Stock is down ~66% from highs and trading near levels not seen in years.

Not call

items.title: Better Buy: Sandisk or Nvidia Stock?
items.body: Sandisk has surged 4,500% in the past year on strong NAND memory demand, outpacing Nvidia's growth rate. However, the article concludes Nvidia is the better long-term investment due to its ability to control product pricing and maintain competitive advantages, while Sandisk's business is more cyclic
items.author: The Motley Fool

items.title: A new wave of weight-loss therapies aims to be better than today’s GLP-1s
items.body: Retatrutide is one of those drugs. It helped people lose a massive amount of weight in clinical trials. It also improved sleep apnea and knee pain.

items.title: S&P 500 sees $1.8 trillion wipeout, Nasdaq tallies biggest point drop on record: What investors need to know about Friday’s selloff
items.body: A remarkable two-month sprint higher for major stock-market indexes encountered its first major hiccup on Friday as the Nasdaq Composite plummeted more than 1,121 points — the biggest one day point drop on record, according to Dow Jones Market Data.
